topic.what: Secure token-based replay conformance checking using homomorphic encryption
topic.how: Reformulated token-based replay using matrix/vector operations (marking equation incidence matrix) compatible with fully homomorphic encryption enabling secure computation between trace owner and model owner
topic.why_matters: Enables conformance checking over encrypted event logs without revealing sensitive process data to other parties critical for cross-organizational scenarios

characteristics.what_it_measures: Time to complete secure token-based replay on encrypted data
characteristics.value: 19.3-37.4s (SEC+ with token counts) 7.8-15.8s (SEC without token counts)
characteristics.evidence: Section 4 evaluation on MacBook Air M1 2020 16GB RAM
characteristics.context: Synthetic event log with traces 4-13 events; comparison between secure and clear versions

characteristics.what_it_measures: Fitness metric computed from token counts
characteristics.value: 1.0 for fitting traces lower for unfitting
characteristics.context: Defined in Definition 6 using missing tokens m and remaining tokens r

characteristics.what_it_measures: Integer bit-width used for encrypted computations
characteristics.vs_baseline: Plaintext operations use native integer representation
characteristics.evidence: Section 4 compiler tunes to 3-bit when upper bound provided
characteristics.context: Affects execution time significantly; 16-bit caused 35-84 min execution

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of places and transitions in Petri net
characteristics.vs_baseline: Matrix dimensions determine O(rho x kappa) complexity
characteristics.evidence: Figure 1 and Figure 2 show net structure
characteristics.context: Complexity dominated by matrix multiplication E with rho enablements and kappa = |P|+|T| columns

good_for: cross-organizational conformance checking where data privacy required
scenarios where both model and log must remain confidential
not_for: large-scale event logs with thousands of traces
requires: Zama's Concrete framework or equivalent FHE library
preprocessed Petri net matrices and vectors

novelty: first secure token-based replay using homomorphic encryption
reformulation of token-based replay via marking equation and matrix operations
client-server architecture for privacy-preserving conformance checking
integration with modern FHE frameworks enabling practical performance
open_problems: evaluation with real-life event logs to assess scalability
addressing token flooding problem in secure setting
re-implementation on other FHE platforms for performance comparison
optimization of integer precision vs execution time tradeoffs
